subject: /home/budi/dev/prestashop-module/.claude/skills/psm-validate
verdict: Lapis 4 (browser E2E) is well-architected and reuses flashlight orchestration cleanly, but a real honest-degrade leak in the aggregate lets a co-tenant browser's launch failure silently void another browser's conclusive blocking finding — a false pass that violates the skill's core promise.
summary: The four validation layers compose symmetrically and the E2E layer's self-contained design, sibling-import reuse, and per-version/per-browser findings are sound and thoroughly unit-tested. The primary opportunity is correctness: honest-degrade is applied at whole-version granularity, so partial per-browser failure (launch-fail or probe-miss) is conflated with version-level infra failure — dropping real blocking findings in one direction and silently skipping promised coverage in the other. Secondary opportunities are trimming/carving the over-budget SKILL.md and healing 'three layer' references left stale by the edit.

themes.title: Per-browser honest-degrade leak (false pass + silent coverage drop)
themes.root_cause: Honest-degrade is applied at whole-version granularity. A per-browser launch failure is appended to the same version-level `errors` list that ps-aggregate.e2e_layer treats as infra, so any non-empty errors zeroes out the version's real blocking findings and marks it not_conclusive → a conclusive fatal caught by one browser escapes because another browser failed to launch. The mirror defect: a probe-missed engine (binary absent) is dropped in main() with no note, so the headline 'Chromium+Firefox' coverage silently runs one browser and still reports conclusive-pass.
themes.action: Keep per-browser launch/probe outcomes OUT of the version-level `errors` infra channel; track them as per-engine coverage notes. In e2e_layer, treat the version as conclusive when >=1 browser ran and the module installed, evaluate its findings as blocking, and surface uncovered engines as an inconclusive note rather than a reason to discard findings. Fall to not_conclusive only when NO engine ran. Add regression tests for both shapes.

themes.title: Verdict under-surfaces silently-skipped work
themes.root_cause: discover_scenarios correctly skips malformed authored specs with a note (result.scenario_notes), but neither ps-aggregate nor the SKILL Vonis guidance propagates it — a typo'd tests/e2e/*.json never runs yet the version reports 'E2E passed', false confidence in the exact authored-coverage feature Lapis 4 adds. Same class as the skipped-engine drop above.
themes.action: Propagate scenario_notes (and skipped-engine coverage) into the E2E layer as an inconclusive-style note, and add one line in SKILL Vonis telling the model to name skipped authored specs / engines in the human summary.

themes.title: Lapis 4 prose over-budget and narrating script internals
themes.root_cause: The Lapis 4 section re-teaches the deterministic pipeline that ps-e2e-run.py --help/docstring and the emitted JSON already own (and the model never re-judges), and the headless no-pull rule is restated three times. Combined with the four inline layer descriptions this pushed SKILL.md to 2697 tokens, over the run's 2500 ceiling.
themes.action: Slim Lapis 4 to its non-inferables (gate, invocation + config->flag wiring, one-time prerequisite, honest-degrade rule), drop the duplicated headless parenthetical, and — if still over budget — carve the four layer descriptions to references/validation-layers.md with a routing pointer.

themes.title: 'Three layer' references left stale by the edit
themes.root_cause: The skill became four layers but self-descriptions weren't all updated: the frontmatter description still enumerates only static/flashlight/adversarial, and ps-aggregate.py's module docstring + argparse description still say 'tiga lapis' though the code composes four (and SKILL routes the model to read script --help).
themes.action: Add the browser/E2E layer to the frontmatter description (or drop the enumeration), and update ps-aggregate.py docstring + argparse to four layers naming Lapis 4.

themes.title: psm_e2e_enabled gate produced but not enforced
themes.root_cause: On Activation reads psm_e2e_enabled as a gate ('absen = aktif') but no execution section instructs skip-when-false at the Lapis 4 run site; the run gates only on Docker + browser availability.
themes.action: State enforcement at the Lapis 4 run site ('skip Lapis 4 when psm_e2e_enabled is false'); registering the key in the resolver stays with the deferred psm-setup step.

strengths: Four validation layers compose symmetrically in ps-aggregate: e2e_layer mirrors flashlight_layer/adversarial_layer, merge_version collects blocking from all four, e2e_conclusive/layers_run.e2e tracked.
Self-contained E2E orchestration reuses ps-flashlight-run.py by sibling import with a backward-compatible optional publish port (no duplication, existing tests intact) — deliberate DRY, not fragile.

Honest-degrade taxonomy at the all-or-nothing boundary is thorough and well-tested (skipped / skipped_image / skipped_browser / infra / install-fail), and proven on a real PS 9.1 run with clean teardown.
Determinism boundary holds in SKILL prose: the model runs scripts and is explicitly forbidden from re-judging pass/e2e_conclusive; no script embeds e-commerce judgment (that stays Lapis 3).
Config surface is a single coherent resolver-based mechanism; no customize.toml, correctly (a per-skill one would duplicate shared family config).

recommendations.action: Fix the per-browser honest-degrade leak: route per-browser launch/probe outcomes to a coverage-note channel (not version `errors`); in e2e_layer make the version conclusive when >=1 browser ran + install ok, evaluating findings as blocking, with uncovered engines as an inconclusive note. Add regression tests for 'one browser blocks while a co-tenant launch-fails' and 'probe-missed engine -> pass with coverage note'.
Propagate scenario_notes (skipped authored specs) and skipped-engine coverage into the E2E layer note and the SKILL Vonis human-summary guidance.
Trim Lapis 4 to non-inferables and drop the duplicated headless parenthetical (uses leanness-1 proposed_smallest); recount tokens and carve to references/ only if still over the intended budget.
Heal stale self-descriptions: add E2E to the frontmatter description and update ps-aggregate.py docstring/argparse to four layers.
State skip-when-false enforcement for psm_e2e_enabled at the Lapis 4 run site.

journeys.name: Interactive validate
journeys.steps: resolve config -> pick module -> run 4 layers (static always; flashlight/E2E if Docker+browser; adversarial always) -> aggregate -> human summary with honest not-conclusive callouts

journeys.name: Headless CI gate
journeys.steps: module-path+versions from args -> run available layers without --allow-image-pull -> JSON only -> exit on overall pass

experience.headless: Solid: skips clarification, never auto-pulls images or downloads browsers, degrades to available layers; needs the skipped-engine/spec coverage note so a partial run isn't mistaken for full coverage.

findings.title: Aggregate drops conclusive E2E blocking findings when a co-tenant browser's launch note lands in errors — honest-degrade produces a false pass
findings.evidence: ps-e2e-run.py drives browsers in a loop: a browser that fails btype.launch() appends a note to the SAME res['errors'] list and continue's, while a browser that DID launch can still return real conclusive blocking findings via assemble_findings. The aggregate then does infra = list(v.get('errors', [])) and if infra: return {state: not_conclusive, findings: []}. So when chromium launches and finds a genuine assertion failure but firefox fails to launch (a common CI shape — missing system libs), the non-empty errors from firefox trips the blanket infra gate and discards chromium's blocking error, marking the version not_conclusive (non-blocking) -> version PASSES. This breaks the conclusive-blocks/infra-not-conclusive contract the aggregate must enforce as the single source of truth.
findings.recommendation: Keep per-browser launch failures out of the fatal errors channel the aggregate treats as infra — put continued-past per-browser notes in a separate field (browser_notes), reserving errors for whole-version infra that returns early. Equivalently, in e2e_layer gate on conclusiveness first: if >=1 browser ran and module installed, the run IS conclusive — evaluate findings as blocking and treat per-browser notes as advisory. Add a unit test asserting one-launched-failing + one-launch-error yields state=fail.

findings.title: Make honest-degrade per-browser — one engine's launch failure suppresses another's real findings, and a missing engine is silently dropped
findings.location: scripts/ps-e2e-run.py run_one_version drive loop (~500-518) + scripts/ps-aggregate.py e2e_layer (~131-141) + SKILL.md 'Lapis 4' / 'Vonis dan output'
findings.evidence: Honest-degrade is applied at whole-browser-set granularity: skipped_browser fires only when zero engines run, and a version is conclusive whenever >=1 engine runs. Leak in two directions. (1) LAUNCH-FAIL over-degrade: chromium blocking finding + firefox launch() throw -> firefox error in res['errors'] AND chromium finding kept -> e2e_layer sees non-empty errors -> not_conclusive with findings:[] -> a shop-breaking behavioral fatal only browser-render catches escapes the gate. (2) PROBE-MISS under-honesty: firefox binary absent -> main() drops it from usable, version drives chromium only with empty errors and reports conclusive-pass; nothing compares requested vs run engines -> the 'Chromium+Firefox' coverage is silently dropped with no note (test 10b: browsers:['chromium'] -> e2e_conclusive True). Shared root cause: all-or-nothing, not per-engine.
findings.recommendation: Carry requested engines into each version result; record requested-but-not-run engines (probe-miss OR launch-fail) as per-engine coverage notes, NOT version errors. In e2e_layer keep the version conclusive on engines that actually ran and attach an inconclusive_note naming skipped engines; not_conclusive only when NO engine ran. Add tests: 'chromium block + firefox launch-fail -> still blocks' and 'firefox probe-miss -> pass but note names firefox'. In SKILL Vonis, tell the model to surface skipped-engine coverage.

findings.title: Surface silently-skipped authored E2E specs in the verdict — a broken tests/e2e/*.json is dropped with no honest signal
findings.location: scripts/ps-e2e-run.py discover_scenarios (~167-188) + main scenario_notes -> not consumed by scripts/ps-aggregate.py e2e_layer + SKILL.md 'Vonis dan output'
findings.evidence: discover_scenarios skips malformed specs (bad JSON / no 'steps') with a note recorded in result['scenario_notes'], but e2e_layer never reads scenario_notes and SKILL Vonis only routes the model to e2e_conclusive + the inconclusive list. So a typo'd tests/e2e/checkout.json means only the universal smoke runs, the version reports 'E2E passed conclusive', and the author's intended behavioral check silently never executed — false confidence in the authored-coverage feature. The honest-degrade ethos is applied to browsers/containers but not to the authored-spec discovery path.
findings.recommendation: Have e2e_layer read the e2e result's scenario_notes and, when any authored spec was skipped, emit an inconclusive-style note ('N authored E2E spec dilewati'). Add one line in SKILL Vonis instructing the model to name skipped authored specs in the human summary.

findings.title: Lapis 4 paragraph narrates ps-e2e-run.py internals the model never acts on
findings.evidence: Between the invocation and the honest-degrade rule, the paragraph re-teaches what the script does deterministically (build DB+flashlight, install, drive per browser, smoke + authored scenarios, blocking rule, BO-without-login inconclusive) plus the meta-line 'Peran lapis dijaga: install/coding-standard divonis Lapis 2, Lapis 4 memvonis perilaku.' All of this is script behavior surfaced by ps-e2e-run.py --help/docstring and the emitted JSON; the aggregate owns conclusiveness and Vonis already forbids re-judging e2e_conclusive. These lines change none of the model's moves (gate -> invoke -> don't re-judge -> reflect skips).
findings.recommendation: Slim to non-inferables — the gate, the exact invocation + config->flag wiring, the one-time prerequisite, and the honest-degrade rule — and let the script's --help and JSON carry the pipeline/verdict mechanics. Drop the 'Peran lapis dijaga' meta-line.
findings.proposed_smallest: **Lapis 4 — E2E browser di Chromium+Firefox (bila Docker + browser Playwright ada).** Prasyarat sekali: `playwright install chromium firefox`. Jalankan `uv run scripts/ps-e2e-run.py <module-path> --versions <target> --browsers <psm_e2e_browsers>` dengan setelan flashlight sama seperti Lapis 2 (`--db-image`, `--ps-domain`, `--orchestrator`, `--startup-timeout`); URL yang di-drive memakai ulang `psm_flashlight_ps_domain` (bukan key baru). Lihat `--help` untuk format spec skenario. Skrip men-drive core asli dan memvonis perilaku; jangan nilai ulang outputnya. Degrade jujur seperti Lapis 2: Playwright/Docker/image/browser absen atau PS tak terjangkau -> versi itu tak konklusif, vonis jatuh ke lapis lain — jangan sembunyikan.
findings.predicted_delta: Nothing that changes the model's actions: the DB+flashlight build, per-browser FO/BO smoke, block-on-assertion and BO-without-login-inconclusive rules are all performed by the script and reported in its JSON, which Vonis forbids overriding. The tests/e2e/*.json authoring convention is a contract for whoever writes scenarios (psm-scaffold/psm-develop), not the validator that only runs them. Route to variant eval to confirm no verdict-quality loss.

findings.title: Headless 'no --allow-image-pull' rule restated across three sections
findings.evidence: The headless-never-pulls rule and its skip tokens appear three times: Lapis 2, Lapis 4's parenthetical '(headless jalan tanpa --allow-image-pull)', and Mode headless. The Lapis 4 parenthetical is a pure restatement of what Mode headless authoritatively owns, in one always-co-loaded SKILL.md.
findings.recommendation: Let Mode headless own the pull-gating rule and its skip tokens; drop the Lapis 4 parenthetical. Keep Lapis 2's mention only for its interactive-vs-headless distinction, which is not restated elsewhere.

findings.title: Lapis 4 edit pushed SKILL.md over the run's token budget with no references/ valve
findings.evidence: prepass-metrics reports SKILL.md at 2697 tiktoken tokens against the enforced budget of 2500 (budgets.skill_md_tokens=[1500,2500]); the pre-edit skill was ~1608-1940 tokens, so the Lapis 4 section plus E2E clauses crossed the tier. The skill has no references/ directory — all four layer descriptions are inline. Note: if the org's canonical budget is the principles default (3000) rather than the 2500 this run enforces, this is a non-issue.
findings.recommendation: Confirm the intended budget; if 2500, bring SKILL.md under it — carve the four Lapis descriptions to references/validation-layers.md with a one-line routing pointer, or trim the E2E prose in place (see leanness-1).

findings.title: Frontmatter description still enumerates three layers after the skill became four
findings.evidence: Overview now says 'empat lapis' and describes the browser/E2E layer, but the frontmatter description still lists only 'pindai statis, uji flashlight, dan review adversarial' — the new E2E/browser layer is omitted. Trigger phrases are unaffected so activation isn't broken, but the enumerated scope is stale.
findings.recommendation: Add the browser/E2E layer to the description summary (or drop the technique enumeration) so it matches 'empat lapis'.

findings.title: ps-aggregate docstring and --help still say 'tiga lapis' while the code composes four
findings.evidence: The code fully wires the fourth layer (e2e_layer, merge_version pulls blocking from static/flashlight/adversarial/e2e, layers_run.e2e, e2e_conclusive), but the module docstring reads 'Satukan tiga lapis validasi ...' and the CLI description is 'Satukan tiga lapis validasi jadi vonis terstruktur.' SKILL.md directs the model to consult script --help, so a caller reading it sees three layers.
findings.recommendation: Update the module docstring and argparse description to four layers, naming Lapis 4 / ps-e2e-run.py.

findings.title: psm_e2e_enabled gate is read in On Activation but no execution section enforces skip-when-false
findings.location: SKILL.md:20 (On Activation) vs SKILL.md Lapis 4 and Mode headless
findings.evidence: On Activation reads psm_e2e_enabled as a gate ('absen = aktif') but the Lapis 4 run instruction gates only on 'Docker + browser Playwright ada' and never references the enabled flag; Mode headless omits it too. So the gate is produced but no later section consumes it at the decision point. The resolver currently emits no psm_e2e_* keys, so the guard degrades gracefully today; but once the flag is false, the model has no explicit instruction to skip Lapis 4.
findings.recommendation: State enforcement at the Lapis 4 run site ('skip Lapis 4 when psm_e2e_enabled is false'); add the two e2e keys to the resolver/config producer in the deferred psm-setup sync step.
